linux-gfx90a.blocked_reason: Scoped libcudf core COMPILES for gfx90a (79 TUs, real gfx90a code object) and a focused GPU gtest for the wave64 null-mask path passes 4/4 on MI250X (count_set_bits/segmented/create_null_mask/determinism). cuCollections is now ported (no longer a blocker) and the foundational column core (column_device_view.cu etc., which the prior run could not compile at all) now builds clean. Macro-cascade VERDICT: the 1-line CUDF_HOST_DEVICE fix (types.hpp, |+__HIPCC__) DID clear the 71-header __host__/__device__ cascade; the only residual was type_dispatcher base_type_to_id specializations needing CUDF_HOST_DEVICE to match the primary (clang strictness). Next concrete walls to a FULLY-linking core + broad GPU suite (the type-erased dispatch link-closure that column.cu/column_factories.cu pull): (1) rocPRIM radix sort rejects cuda::std::tuple keys (extract_digit/decode_inplace/tuple_size gap) in sort_radix.cu/sorted_order_radix.cu; (2) cuda::std::array<unsigned,100> absent from the vendored libhipcxx (strings/case.cu); (3) unconverted wave64 ballots in broader strings/json/merge/rolling/join kernels; then (4) the jitify/NVRTC JIT wall (binaryop/transform/rolling) and the now-reachable cuco-backed algorithms (join/groupby/hash/distinct). src/io (nvCOMP/KvikIO) stays scoped out. See notes.md.
